core_mechanism: a phase transition at the marginal (lower-critical) dimension where fluctuations forbid true long-range order yet a topological defect-unbinding (Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless-type) transition still separates a disordered phase from a low-temperature phase carrying quasi-long-range, power-law-decaying order
brief_reason: The abstract stresses that in the marginal 2d case thermal fluctuations destroy the fully ordered state but not the coherent (superfluid) response, leaving residual 'quasi-long-range' order at low temperatures — the signature of a topological transition into a power-law-ordered phase at the lower critical dimension.
